# Test fixtures

Short media files used by `cargo test`, `scripts/test-e2e.sh`, and ad-hoc
manual streaming. All licensed for unrestricted use; see Attribution below.

| File | Container | Video | Audio | Duration | Use |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `loop-3s.mp4` | mp4 | H.264 baseline 320×240 @15fps, single-slice | AAC-LC 48k mono, 440 Hz sine | 3 s | Smallest possible sanity fixture; the unit tests in `src/ffmpeg/pipeline.rs` reference this file by name |
| `motion-pattern-5s.mp4` | mp4 | H.264 baseline 512×512 @15fps, single-slice (ffmpeg `mptestsrc`) | AAC-LC 48k mono, harmonic chord (440/554/659 Hz) | 5 s | Motion + harmony — verifies P-frame handling and that audio isn't a pure single tone |
| `smptebars-30fps-stereo-5s.mp4` | mp4 | H.264 baseline 480×270 @30fps, single-slice (SMPTE color bars) | AAC-LC 48k **stereo**, 1 kHz sine | 5 s | Tests stereo audio path + 30 fps video; visual is the SMPTE EG-1 bars |
| `bbb-30s.mp4` | mp4 | H.264 baseline 320×180 @24fps, single-slice (re-encoded) | AAC-LC 48k mono | 30 s | Real-world content with recognizable visuals and animal-sound audio. Source: *Big Buck Bunny*. CC-BY 3.0 |
| `sintel-15s.mp4` | mp4 | H.264 baseline 480×270 @24fps, single-slice (re-encoded) | AAC-LC 48k stereo | 15 s | Real-world content with music + dialog. Source: *Sintel* (Durian Open Movie Project). CC-BY 3.0 |
| `hevc-opus-5s.mp4` | mp4 | H.265/HEVC 320×240 @15fps, keyint 15 (ffmpeg `testsrc`) | Opus 48k mono, 440 Hz sine | 5 s | Exercises the HEVC + Opus encoded-passthrough path (`parse::hevc` / `parse::opus`, `-f hevc` / `-f ogg`) |
| `probe-*.json` | JSON | — | — | — | Sample `ffprobe -of json` outputs used by `src/agora/publisher.rs` unit tests |

## Why everything is re-encoded `slices=1:profile=baseline:yuv420p`

Three constraints from the encoded passthrough path:

1. **Single slice per frame.** Our `parse::h264::next_au` walker treats every
   VCL slice as the start of a new access unit. Sources that split a picture
   across many slices (e.g. ffmpeg's default x264 settings, or the public
   Sintel trailer) get fragmented into 24–800 byte "frames" that the SDK
   packetizer can't reassemble.
2. **Constrained Baseline profile, yuv420p.** Agora's encoded video sender
   advertises one profile to subscribers; mismatched profile / chroma
   subsampling shows up as decoder errors on the receiving side.
3. **Periodic keyframes.** `-x264-params keyint=…` so subscribers joining
   mid-stream don't wait forever for the first IDR.

  `smptebars-30fps-stereo-5s.mp4`, `hevc-opus-5s.mp4`) were generated by
  `ffmpeg` from `lavfi` filter sources (`testsrc`, `mptestsrc`,
  `smptebars`, `sine`, `aevalsrc`).
  No copyright; reproduce them by replaying the commands in
  `scripts/regen-fixtures.sh` (see below).

## Regenerating fixtures

`scripts/regen-fixtures.sh` rebuilds every file in this directory from the
exact `ffmpeg` invocations recorded there. Run it after any change to the
fixture format expectations (slice count, profile, audio config, …) to
keep the table above honest.
